title: Error: creating named volume "~/.aws": running volume create option: names must match [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*
body:
### Issue Description

Trying use AWS Glue 4.0 with podman - based on the documentation here - https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/aws-glue-programming-etl-libraries.html#develop-local-docker-image

The following command works perfectly with docker - 
docker run -it -v ~/.aws:/home/glue_user/.aws -e DISABLE_SSL=true --rm -p 4040:4040 -p 18080:18080 --name glue_pyspark amazon/aws-glue-libs:glue_libs_4.0.0_image_01 pyspark

with podman it fails with Error: creating named volume "~/.aws": running volume create option: names must match [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*: invalid argument

podman run -it -v ~/.aws:/home/glue_user/.aws -e DISABLE_SSL=true --rm -p 4040:4040 -p 18080:18080 --name glue_pyspark amazon/aws-glue-libs:glue_libs_4.0.0_image_01 pyspark

if I omit the volume flag it works

podman run -it -e DISABLE_SSL=true --rm -p 4040:4040 -p 18080:18080 --name glue_pyspark amazon/aws-glue-libs:glue_libs_4.0.0_image_01 pyspark

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman machine start
2. podman pull amazon/aws-glue-libs:glue_libs_4.0.0_image_01
3. podman run --log-level=debug -it -v ~/.aws:/home/glue_user/.aws -e DISABLE_SSL=true --rm -p 4040:4040 -p 18080:18080 --name glue_pyspark amazon/aws-glue-libs:glue_libs_4.0.0_image_01 pyspark



### Describe the results you received

time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="Called run.PersistentPreRunE(podman run --log-level=debug -it -v ~/.aws:/home/glue_user/.aws -e DISABLE_SSL=true --rm -p 4040:4040 -p 18080:18080 --name glue_pyspark amazon/aws-glue-libs:glue_libs_4.0.0_image_01 pyspark)"
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.4.2/libpod/_ping"
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.4.2/libpod/networks/pasta/exists"
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="Adding port mapping from 4040 to 4040 length 1 protocol \"\""
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="Adding port mapping from 18080 to 18080 length 1 protocol \"\""
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="Loading registries configuration \"/etc/containers/registries.conf\""
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v4.4.2/libpod/images/pull"

time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v4.4.2/libpod/containers/create"
Error: creating named volume "~/.aws": running volume create option: names must match [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*: invalid argument
time="2023-03-19T18:29:02Z" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"

### Describe the results you expected

PySpark prompt

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I can not repeat this failure, are you still seeing it in podman 4.5?

> podman [..] -v ~/.aws:/home/glue_user/.aws [..]

Can you try with an absolute path? Using a relative path turned out to be causing an apparently similar issue for me.
Are these with older versions of Podman?  I believe this is fixed in podman 4.5.

> Are these with older versions of Podman? I believe this is fixed in podman 4.5.

In my case podman 3.0.1+dfsg1-3+deb11u1, latest version in Debian stable. On the bright side, there are good chances people hitting this will find this issue here, and see the workaround.

I think I have the same problem, but stating a full path doesnt work for me.

RHEL9 has,

[root@vuwunicosftp002 2222]# podman --version
podman version 4.4.1
